heading: Today
text: Periods of sun giving way to thickening clouds, spreading south, with showers developing through Quebec midday, working south to the Adirondacks and Rt. 4 during the afternoon, reaching southern areas later in the day.  Highs in the 60s, a few low 70s far south.  Winds light, becoming southwest near 10 mph east, and west 10 to 15 mph from the Green Mountains west, gusting 25 to 40 mph, strongest in NY.

heading: Tonight
text: Rain and showers from the Adirondacks, and I-89 east and north, and a good chance southwest.  Lows in the low to mid 40s, a few upper 30s over higher elevations north.  Winds shifting to the northeast 10 to 15 mph, gusting to 30 mph.

heading: Saturday
text: Rain and showers, mostly south of the Adirondacks and Rt. 2, diminishing north to south.  Some breaks of morning sun north, spreading south during the afternoon. Chilly and raw.  Highs only in the 50s to low 60s.  Winds northeast, becoming north 10 to 15 mph, gusting to 30 mph, decreasing in the afternoon.
